title: T-118 — UI: Settings toggle for `require_grn_qc_inspection` (Quality section placeholder)
prompt:
# T-118 — UI: Settings toggle for `require_grn_qc_inspection` (Quality section placeholder)

## Goal
Settings UI toggle for require_grn_qc_inspection + Server Action + audit_log + i18n keys.

PO decision 2026-05-03: T-117/T-118 quality flag placeholders remain in Settings. New /settings/quality section card. Server Action wraps T-117 accessor setter. RBAC plus audit plus i18n keys. Banner notes full enforcement ships with 09-QUALITY.

## Files in scope
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/quality/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/quality/_components/RequireGrnQcToggle.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/quality/_actions/setRequireGrnQcInspection.ts [create]
- apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/quality/_components/__tests__/RequireGrnQcToggle.test.tsx [create]
- apps/web/messages/en/02-settings.json [edit]
- apps/web/messages/pl/02-settings.json [edit]

## Acceptance criteria (all must hold)
- Given the page loads with flag=false, when rendered, then the toggle is OFF and helper text + 'Coming with Quality module' banner are visible.
- Given the user toggles ON, when Save settles, then setRequireGrnQcInspection is called with true and an audit_log entry exists.
- Given a user without settings.quality.write, when on the page, then the toggle is disabled with tooltip.
- Given i18n keys are added, when 02-settings namespace test runs, then PL + EN parity holds.
- Given `pnpm vitest run` for the toggle test + namespace test, then 0 failures.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED RTL tests for default render, toggle save path, RBAC disabled, audit emission (mock).
- RED i18n parity test (re-uses T-116 namespace test).
- Run `pnpm vitest run apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/quality && pnpm vitest run apps/web/messages/__tests__/02-settings.namespace.test.ts`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement GRN-side enforcement.
- Do not bypass withOrgContext or audit_log.
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui.

## Prototype reference
**Source:** `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/ops-screens.jsx:166-198`
**Label:** `features_screen`
**Component type:** form

> **No exact prototype** — closest visual pattern: `features_screen` at `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/settings/ops-screens.jsx:166-198`. Build in the same structural style as this screen. Use a single `sg-row` (label + hint + Toggle) inside one `Section` for the require_grn_qc_inspection flag, following the exact prototype density and the Premium/Beta-style badge pattern for a 'Coming with Quality module' banner.

### Parity requirements (mandatory ACs)
The production implementation MUST match the prototype on three dimensions — add these as explicit ACs:
1. **Structural parity** — same sections, same field labels, same shadcn primitives invoked, same modals triggered from same actions.
2. **Visual parity** — same density tokens, same shadcn/Radix primitives (no drift to plain HTML), same layout proportions.
3. **Interaction parity** — same enable/disable rules, same loading/empty/error states, same keyboard focus order.

### Translation notes (from prototype-labels index)
- window.SETTINGS_FEATURES list → Drizzle query on feature_flags table filtered by scope='module' and plan eligibility
- Toggle per feature → Server Action for feature_flags update with RBAC Admin guard; pre-flight checks for dependent flags
- Premium/Beta badges → derived from feature_flags.plan_tier and feature_flags.beta fields
- Early access link → routes to /settings/admin/flags for full feature flag admin (FlagsAdminScreen)
- Plan check ('Premium plan') → org.plan_tier from orgs table; gates which features are toggleable
- Hardcoded labels → next-intl keys

### shadcn primitives to use
`Switch`, `Badge`, `Card`, `Button`

pipeline_inputs.description: Settings UI toggle for require_grn_qc_inspection + Server Action + audit_log + i18n keys.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Use settings.flags.edit; do not introduce settings.quality.*.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Toggle and namespace tests cover EN/PL parity and permission behavior.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
acceptance_criteria: Flag=false renders toggle OFF with Quality-coming banner.
User with settings.flags.edit can save true and audit_log records change.
User without settings.flags.edit sees disabled/read-only toggle; no settings.quality.* permission is referenced.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Toggle and namespace tests cover EN/PL parity and permission behavior.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: RED RTL tests for default render, toggle save path, RBAC disabled, audit emission (mock).
RED i18n parity test (re-uses T-116 namespace test).
Run `pnpm vitest run apps/web/app/[locale]/(admin)/settings/quality && pnpm vitest run apps/web/messages/__tests__/02-settings.namespace.test.ts`.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not bypass withOrgContext or audit_log.
pipeline_inputs.prototype_index_entry: flags_admin_screen (closest pattern — per-tenant boolean toggle; SET settings.flags.edit)
